title: Eva 30 Q Runtime is 106 Minutes
wikitext:
[]: Eva 3.0 (Q) Runtime is 106 Minutes             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Nov 12, 2012 8:48 am Eva 3.0 (Q) Runtime is 106 Minutes (1 hour and 46 minutes): http://www.evageeks.org/2012/11/eva-3-0-runtime-106-minutes/ Sauce with that pasta: http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-1896.html Wonder how much longer the 3.33 DVD/BD version would be by comparison.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Nov 12, 2012 8:52 am Is that including the God Warrior Short?

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Mon Nov 12, 2012 8:57 am That's about as long as 2.0, I'm kinda disappointed, where's my 150 minutes epic Eva movie?

[]:             Quote Post by tomfrom994 » Mon Nov 12, 2012 9:47 am 1 hour and 46 minutes? That's a sexy sounding runtime. This entire time I was thinking that it would end up being 40 minutes long. (Also, I know I haven't been on in a few days, but what's with the moustaches?)

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Mon Nov 12, 2012 10:01 am I kind of suspected it would be full-length release since Q and Final are now separate movies. But I admit that also I felt some risk that they would be one movie, only released in two half-length pieces. So I guess it's good. The moustaches are a Movember thing. Google.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Mon Nov 12, 2012 10:34 am Well...this is disappointing. I wonder who's going to get shafted in the characterization department this time around?

[]:             Quote Post by Amitch1991 » Mon Nov 12, 2012 10:35 am That's pretty much the length i thought it would be (between 1hr 35 and 1hr 50 mins) hopefully they pack in as much action as possible ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Mon Nov 12, 2012 10:40 am Does this include the God Warrior short film? I hoped that 3.0 could be around 120 minutes.....

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Nov 12, 2012 10:49 am Amitch1991 wrote: That's pretty much the length i thought it would be (between 1hr 35 and 1hr 50 mins) hopefully they pack in as much action as possible ;)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 12, 2012 10:57 am Dima wrote: I hoped that 3.0 could be around 120 minutes.....

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Nov 12, 2012 11:13 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Me too, however I must admit, a two hour animated film can be really draining.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Nov 12, 2012 12:15 pm How long were the others, (initial & BD-Version), for a comparision? 2.0 was already thickly-packed, I remember constantly going "What, there's still so much of this left?" when I first watched it. So I'd trust Anno to use all 106 minutes to their fullest.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Nov 12, 2012 1:36 pm Sounds like a solid runtime to me. The movie has a lot of material to cover but 2.0 got through most of the necessary action. 1.0 was about 95 minutes theatrical & 2.0 was about 108.

[]: Re: Eva 3.0 (Q) Runtime is 106 Minutes             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Nov 12, 2012 6:11 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Wonder how much longer the 3.33 DVD/BD version would be by comparison.

[]: Re: Eva 3.0 (Q) Runtime is 106 Minutes             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Nov 12, 2012 6:21 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: Did 1.11, and 2.22 add additional scenes that prolonged the movie?

[]: Re: Eva 3.0 (Q) Runtime is 106 Minutes             Quote Post by FluffyBunny » Mon Nov 12, 2012 6:22 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: I wasn't able to see 1.0 or 2.0 in theaters. Did 1.11, and 2.22 add additional scenes that prolonged the movie?

[]: Re: Eva 3.0 (Q) Runtime is 106 Minutes             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Nov 12, 2012 6:26 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: I wasn't able to see 1.0 or 2.0 in theaters. Did 1.11, and 2.22 add additional scenes that prolonged the movie?

[]:             Quote Post by jimmiel » Tue Nov 13, 2012 12:40 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Me too, however I must admit, a two hour animated film can be really draining.

[]:             Quote Post by Jadley » Tue Nov 13, 2012 1:44 am jimmiel wrote: I disagree. Unless it's a boring film, I don't see how length in and of itself can be "draining". As long as the length is appropriate for the story, I really think you'd have to get significantly over four hours for me to feel a movie is "too long".

[]:             Quote Post by McDirty » Wed Nov 14, 2012 7:49 pm Jadley wrote: i fully agree, if it's good it's worth it. Like LOTR.
